evidence.text: joining the it consultant industry can be both exhilarating and daunting and choosing a firm like accenture can greatly enhance your career trajectory accenture offers a unique opportunity for new consultants to network with peers and mentors fostering connections that are crucial for professional growth the company s vast reach and diverse portfolio provide an unmatched gateway into various industries granting expo...

exposure to big clients and global projects good learning opportunities with access to many certifications and training programs career growth is structured with clear promotion paths brand value adds weight to resume work-life balance can be decent in some projects

- great exposure to federal clients and large-scale programs - smart collaborative colleagues across functions - strong learning culture and certification support - opportunity to work on emerging tech ai cloud cybersecurity - leadership is supportive of growth
lots of growth steady salary growth if you perform well nice office can meet some great people opportunity to try new things if you know the right people opportunity timing
good benefits lots of opportunity lots of locations worldwide remote work

- highly reputable firm - free food nice office - fairly priced and nice benefits the typical ones but multiple health insurance options - random deals with rental car companies and perks and benefits
good pay easy to sustain good benefits
good pay 401k health benefits
good pay okay benefits remote possibilities
good pay good benefits and a lot of programs to take advantage of there s opportunities to move to different accounts so you aren t stuck doing the same thing
work life balance support benefit perks pto
- good work life balance - great people - great offices - pretty good pto
decent pay benefits good pto 6 401k match discounted stock they pay for certifications work life balance is good on the projects i have been on people are very nice overall lots of remote options
work life balance and flexibility of hours

this is a rare experience i had but i really don t have any complains about my time at accenture i was part of the adobe business group and everything in that group was set up such that i could succeed -great team members -extremely generous referral bonus opportunities 10k before taxes for an l9 -excellent team leaders who choose projects for you -never had to worry about being on the bench -most well-connected con...

enjoy a mix of independent work and teamwork in a supportive collaborative environment you'll work closely with clients find real job satisfaction and be part of a great team that values shared success

new grad working as a consulting development program analyst in chicago pay is 83 200 year 10 000 sign on bonus pre tax work environment is generally good it s tricky to learn to navigate such a large company but there s also a lot of community support mentorship and variety available generally balanced work life and 30 days of pto which people are very respectful of

accenture is an excellent place to kick off your career the company offers great training programs and plenty of opportunities to build your skills before taking on client work i felt well-prepared and confident going into projects thanks to the structured learning paths and hands-on support the work culture was also a big plus my seniors were supportive approachable and respectful which made a huge difference when...

there s a lot of room to grow managers are supportive of the employee s career aspirations free training programs classroom and virtual that are designed to up skill employees continuous learning through mentorship and coaching program competitive benefits committed to keeping a great place to work environment
great learning opportunities and supportive team environment
supportive environment with learning opportunities
great culture and people stable job
lots of project and kinda job security
semi stable job but that s about it
wfh is nice and projects are generally respectful of the 9-5 workday

they pushed our promotion cycle back by 6 months this year haven't been promoted salary has not been raised and have not received any bonus in over a year they also just announced they are cutting diversity and equity programming and recruiting bad vibes all around

frequent travel depending on project high expectations tight timelines stressful at times felt like i was thrown to the wolves majority of the time sink or swim environment feel like pay doesn't equate to amount of work responsibility

- career counseling people lead concept doesn't work well for all - good manager people lead and team is on luck sometimes dte alignment is not appropriate - promotion largely depends on people lead who may or may not represent well in the moderation therefore a good deserving candidate may not get the reward s he is waiting for years - lay offs at top level even for the employees delicately served for decades
searching assignment in between projects is difficult not everybody can build network one has to chase managers and blindly make calls to get projects

accenture could avoid a lot of re-work if it started with the more holistic story it wants to tell clients instead it often builds a granular solution and then figures out the larger story--which often results in disjointed stories and lots of re-work there are pockets of work all weekend mindsets in the company and like any place there are a few rotten apples who do things like steal credit in salesforce for deals...

broken system for chargeability no promotions leadership has no interest in your career growth no bonus no raise they keep acquiring companies to show the pipeline of revenue but the actual numbers don t add up people are back stabby the skill sets and titles of individuals who get acquired are not matched perfectly when they acquire and they tend to rip you off in the process the leadership has absolutely no idea a...
work is contract based so even if you work your tail off you could still end up getting let go if you cannot find a new project
low pay comparing to other big companies not a lot of opportunities for promotion
they worked us a lot hours and didn't pay us very well compared to what they were billing us out at
not good pay they barely raise it
lots of travel bonus not great recently

i would like a better work-life balance overall as with many large companies it's clear that the company does not care about you as an individual for starting roles accenture hires almost exclusively from their intern pool which i think is unfair and greatly limits the talent they could bring in

you have no idea what is going on the amount of times i will set up time with people email etc and receive not even a response when trying to learn more about a project industry etc is outrageous it truly feels like you are in the void when you are not staffed and the anxiety associated with that is outrageous
- pay is not competitive - recognition is poor
promotions bonuses pay increases are in a lull due to the market

one of the hardest questions you'll have to answer is what does accenture do and you can spend 20 years here and not know the full depth and breadth to answer that's how big this company is and while it is hard to feel out of place it is easy to get lost in corporate churn consulting is not for everyone
bad management accenture did not listen
promotion is not clear and you totally depend of the project manager
i was engaging with the team over the summer months so understandably the conversations stretched out over several weeks
salary hasnt increased in years
run as fast as you can and don't look back
too many politics at leadership level
morales is very low hierarchical culture tolerates bad actions from powerful leaders who do not follow ethical standards julie sweet has no strategy
work culture can be toxic
arrogant politics pretentious posturing boring
